input.background: In the late 1970s, the United States male population between 20-29-years of age had body weights with an approximate lognormal distribution with mean $\mu=170$ pounds and standard deviation $\sigma=40$. To test whether body weight has increased since that time, we take an SRS of men from the current population, calculate their mean weight, and compare it to the historical mean.
input.question: Under the null hypothesis, there is no difference in the mean body weights between then and now.

output.answer: Let $\mu$ represent the current population mean. Therefore, the null hypothesis is $H_0: \mu=170$. Under the alternative hypothesis, the mean weight has increased. In other words, $H_{\mathrm{a}}: \mu>170$ pounds.

historical_mean_weight.description: The mean body weight of the United States male population between 20-29 years of age in the late 1970s.

historical_std_dev_weight.description: The standard deviation of body weight of the United States male population between 20-29 years of age in the late 1970s.

current_mean_weight.description: The mean body weight of the current sample of men aged 20-29 years.

meta_info.caption: Null and alternative hypotheses (Body weight of  20-29-year-old U.S. men).
